articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5 in late March 2026, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates significant improvements in long-horizon planning tasks and mathematical proof generation, narrowing the gap with domain-specific systems. Industry benchmarks show a 35% improvement on ARC-AGI-2 compared to its predecessor.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics Models Enable Zero-Shot Manipulation in Unstructured Environments
articles.summary: Google DeepMind published results from its Gemini Robotics program showing foundation models that allow robotic arms to perform complex manipulation tasks — including deformable object handling and tool use — without task-specific fine-tuning. The system leverages a vision-language-action architecture trained on a massive dataset of real-world and simulated interactions. Several logistics partners are beginning pilot deployments in warehouse settings.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude with Real-Time Tool Use and Autonomous Agent Capabilities
articles.summary: Anthropic rolled out a major update to Claude enabling persistent agent loops with real-time tool use, including code execution, web browsing, and file system interaction within sandboxed environments. The system introduces a novel constitutional-AI-based safety layer for agentic actions, requiring explicit user approval for irreversible operations. Early enterprise adopters report significant productivity gains in software engineering and data analysis workflows.

articles.title: Figure and BMW Expand Humanoid Robot Deployment Across Three Additional Manufacturing Plants
articles.summary: Figure announced the expansion of its humanoid robot workforce into three additional BMW manufacturing facilities in Europe, scaling from the initial pilot to over 200 units performing assembly-line tasks such as component insertion, quality inspection, and inter-station transport. The Figure 02 platform now operates with improved uptime of 18+ hours per cycle and demonstrates emergent task adaptation through on-device learning. This marks one of the largest commercial humanoid deployments to date.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm A14 Process Node Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed it has started high-volume manufacturing on its A14 (1.4nm-class) process node at its Kaohsiung fab, roughly one quarter ahead of original projections. The node utilizes gate-all-around nanosheet transistors with backside power delivery, yielding approximately 20% performance improvement and 30% power reduction over N2. Apple and Nvidia are reported to be among the first customers taping out designs on the new process.

articles.title: Meta Releases Llama 4 Behemoth as Open-Weight Model with Mixture-of-Experts Architecture
articles.summary: Meta released Llama 4 Behemoth, a 2-trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model with open weights, making it the largest openly available language model to date. The model activates roughly 400B parameters per forward pass and matches or exceeds several proprietary frontier models on reasoning and code generation benchmarks. The release includes optimized inference kernels for multi-node deployment, lowering the barrier for organizations with modest GPU clusters.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface Implant
articles.summary: Neuralink announced FDA clearance for its N2 implant, a second-generation BCI device featuring 4,096 electrode channels — quadruple the original — and wireless data throughput sufficient for real-time high-bandwidth neural decoding. Clinical trial data from the first cohort showed paralyzed participants achieving cursor control speeds approaching able-bodied benchmarks. The approval covers expanded trials for both motor and communication restoration indications.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Hyundai Unveil Atlas Commercial Platform for Construction Site Autonomy
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics, backed by Hyundai, formally launched the commercial version of its electric Atlas humanoid platform targeted at construction applications, including autonomous rebar carrying, site surveying with integrated LiDAR, and stair navigation with heavy payloads up to 25 kg. The system integrates a vision-language model for natural language task instruction from on-site supervisors. Pre-orders from three major construction firms signal strong industrial demand for humanoid platforms beyond manufacturing.
